prompt: The dangling else attaches to the wrong if. What is the bug?
choices: Nested if statements are not allowed without braces
The else attaches to the nearest if (x > 10), so it prints 'non-positive' when x <= 10 and x > 0, not when x <= 0 as the indentation implies
The compiler rejects dangling else statements
else without braces always attaches to the outermost if
explanation: Java's dangling-else rule: an else attaches to the nearest unmatched if. So the else belongs to 'if (x>10)', not 'if (x>0)'. For x=5: outer if passes, inner if fails → else runs → 'non-positive' prints, even though x is positive. Fix: add braces to explicitly control which if the else belongs to.
